evals.prompt: I just started a new React app with create-react-app and I need to add CleverTap analytics to track user signups and page views. My account ID is TEST-123-456 and I'm on the US region.
evals.expected_output: Should detect Web platform (React variant), load core/integration/web.md, generate npm install + React useEffect init pattern + event tracking code for signups and page views. Should use the provided account ID and us1 region.

assertions.text: Detects React as the web framework variant
Includes event.push for page view tracking

evals.prompt: I have a Swift iOS app and I'm getting no events in my CleverTap dashboard. I added the SDK via CocoaPods last week and put the init code in viewDidLoad of my main view controller. Debug logs show nothing.
evals.expected_output: Should detect iOS platform, enter debug mode, load core/debugging/common.md first (init ordering issue - viewDidLoad is wrong, should be AppDelegate), then core/debugging/ios.md. Should identify that autoIntegrate must be in didFinishLaunchingWithOptions, not viewDidLoad.

assertions.text: Enters debug mode (not integration or audit)
Identifies init placement as the issue (viewDidLoad vs AppDelegate)
Suggests enabling debug logging to verify fix

evals.prompt: We have a large Android app with multiple login flows (email, Google SSO, Facebook). I want to understand where CleverTap is being used across our codebase - which flows call onUserLogin, where we track events, and whether push is set up correctly.
evals.expected_output: Should detect Android platform, enter audit mode, load core/audit/features.md, scan for all CleverTap method calls using the regex patterns, group by feature area (Identity, Events, Push, etc.), and generate the audit report template showing usage across the login flows.

assertions.text: Enters audit mode (not integration or debug)
Searches for onUserLogin calls across the codebase
Groups findings by feature area in the report

evals.prompt: Add CleverTap to my Flutter app. I need event tracking and push notifications on both Android and iOS.
evals.expected_output: Should detect Flutter platform, load core/integration/flutter.md, ask for credentials, generate pubspec.yaml dep + Android manifest + iOS Info.plist + Dart init code + push setup for both platforms.

assertions.text: Asks for Account ID, Token, and Region
Includes Android native setup (manifest + Application class)
Includes push notification setup for both platforms

evals.prompt: I'm getting 429 errors when uploading events to CleverTap from my Node.js backend. I'm sending about 500 events per second using individual API calls.
evals.expected_output: Should detect server/Node platform, enter debug mode, load core/debugging/server.md, identify rate limiting as the issue (15 concurrent requests), recommend batching events (up to 1000 per request) instead of individual calls.

assertions.text: Mentions the 1000 record per batch limit

evals.prompt: I'm building a React Native app and my push notifications work on iOS but not on Android. The app is targeting Android 13+.
evals.expected_output: Should detect React Native, enter debug mode, check Android-specific push issues: notification channels (required for Android 8+), POST_NOTIFICATIONS runtime permission (required for Android 13+), FCM token registration.

assertions.text: Checks notification channel setup for Android 8+
Identifies POST_NOTIFICATIONS permission requirement for Android 13+

evals.prompt: Integrate CleverTap into my Rust microservice for server-side event tracking.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize Rust is not a supported platform with a reference file, gracefully handle by asking the developer for more context, and fallback to fetching from developer.clevertap.com or suggest using the REST API directly (similar to server SDK patterns).

assertions.text: Recognizes Rust is not directly covered
Does not hallucinate a Rust SDK
Suggests REST API approach or fetches from CleverTap docs
Provides useful guidance despite missing reference file

evals.prompt: Our app has both a Podfile and a build.gradle. I want to add CleverTap to it.
evals.expected_output: Should detect ambiguity (could be iOS, Android, or React Native/Flutter monorepo), ask the developer which platform they want to target rather than guessing.

assertions.text: Asks the developer which platform to target
Does not assume a platform without asking
